title: Provider Tool Execution (FileSearch / WebSearch / WebFetch)
key_classes: FileSearch extends ProviderTool — props: stores[], filters[], ids(): array; resolveFilters(Closure|array|null): array
WebSearch extends ProviderTool — props: maxSearches, allowedDomains[], city, region, country; methods: max(), allow(), location(), hasLocation()
WebFetch extends ProviderTool — props: maxSearches, allowedDomains[]; methods: max(), allow()
FileSearchQuery — filter builder; methods: where(), whereNot(), whereIn(), whereNotIn(), toArray()
UrlCitation extends Citation — props: url, title; implements Arrayable, JsonSerializable
Streaming\Events\Citation extends StreamEvent — wraps CitationData; toArray() / toVercelProtocolArray()
Streaming\Events\ProviderToolEvent extends StreamEvent — props: id, itemId, type, data[], status, timestamp
activity_flow: START: Agent loop receives tool list containing ProviderTool instances
FOR EACH tool in tools array:
NO  → throw RuntimeException('Provider does not support file search')
NO  → throw RuntimeException('Provider does not support web search')
YES → call provider->webSearchToolOptions(tool) → build search tool definition
NO  → throw RuntimeException('Provider does not support web fetch')
YES → call provider->webFetchToolOptions(tool) → build fetch tool definition
Anthropic + unknown ProviderTool → throw LogicException('not supported by Anthropic')
NO (regular Tool) → mapTool(tool) → build function/tool definition
SEND request to provider API with mapped tools
extractCitations(output) → iterate output items of type='message'
create UrlCitation(url, title) → push to citations collection
deduplicate citations by title → return unique collection
[Decision] finishReason === ToolCalls AND toolCalls filled AND steps < maxSteps?
YES → executeTool(tool, arguments) → fire invokingToolCallback → tool->handle(Request) → fire toolInvokedCallback → return string result
create ToolResult(id, name, arguments, result, resultId)
call continueWithToolResults() → POST responses with previous_response_id + tool results input
NO → build final TextResponse / StructuredTextResponse with citations in Meta
response.output_text.delta → yield TextDelta (lazy TextStart on first delta)
response.output_item.done (type ends in '_call' but not 'function_call') → yield ProviderToolEvent(id, itemId, type, data, 'completed', ts)
response.*_call.* events (non-function_call) → yield ProviderToolEvent(id, item_id, callType, data, status, ts)
FOR EACH pending toolCall: executeTool() → yield ToolResultEvent
YES → POST responses with stream:true, previous_response_id, tool results → recursively processTextStream()
key_relationships: FileSearch, WebSearch, WebFetch all extend ProviderTool
FileSearch uses FileSearchQuery to build filters (Closure|array → query builder pattern)
Streaming\Events\Citation wraps Responses\Data\Citation; handles UrlCitation subtype via match()
OpenAI\MapsTools handles: FileSearch (→ file_search), WebSearch (→ web_search_preview)
Anthropic\MapsTools handles: WebSearch (→ web_search_20250305), WebFetch (→ web_fetch_20250910); rejects FileSearch with LogicException
Gemini\MapsTools handles: FileSearch (→ fileSearch), WebSearch (→ google_search), WebFetch (→ url_context)
Provider capability gating: SupportsFileSearch, SupportsWebSearch, SupportsWebFetch interfaces checked at map time
InvokesTools::executeTool() drives the tool callback lifecycle (invokingToolCallback → handle → toolInvokedCallback)
important_notes: ProviderTool is NOT a user-invoked Tool (Contract\Tool interface); it configures native provider-side capabilities
ProviderToolEvent is yielded only in streaming for non-function_call provider tool updates (e.g. web_search_call, file_search_call)
Citations are extracted only in non-streaming (ParsesTextResponses::extractCitations); in streaming they come via Streaming\Events\Citation
FileSearch.ids() resolves Store objects to their string IDs at map time
Citations are deduplicated by title before inclusion in Meta
Streaming ProviderToolEvent detection: itemType ends with '_call' but is NOT 'function_call'
